page: Microsoft Azure FX4-2mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 1 physical core, 2 threads per core, and 2 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 260 MB of L3 cache. The server is equipped with 84.0 GB of system memory, yielding 42.0 GB of memory per core. It includes 236 GB of local storage, has no GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a significant divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2342.0 (top 10%) and strong single-threaded performance in gzip compression (34.72 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (14.32 GB/sec). Conversely, multi-core and parallel processing metrics are weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 2884.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 2217.08. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with large-block read-only and write bandwidths categorized as poor at 21.11 GB/sec and 14.3 GB/sec, respectively.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX4-2mds_v2 offers a highly specialized resource density, pairing a massive memory-to-vCPU ratio of 42 GB per core with a high-frequency single-core CPU. This configuration is tailored for workloads that require substantial memory capacity but do not scale across multiple threads, such as memory-intensive single-threaded applications, specific database operations, or text processing tasks. However, the poor memory bandwidth and weak multi-core scaling represent clear tradeoffs for parallel computing, high-throughput web serving, or large-scale Redis deployments.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX4-2mds_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 2 vCPUs, 84.0 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage. Benchmarks show a stark contrast in performance: single-core capabilities are exceptional, with a Geekbench single-core score in the top 10% and strong single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core performance is weak, and large-block memory bandwidth is poor, measuring 21.11 GB/sec for reads. This server is designed for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads, small-scale database operations, and text processing, where massive memory capacity per core is required but multi-threaded scaling is not a priority.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX4-2mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 2 vCPUs, 84.0 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance alongside weak multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX4-2mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 2 vCPUs, 84.0 GB RAM, and 236 GB local storage, offering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-intensive server combining exceptional single-core clock speeds with an ultra-high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 2 vCPUs at 4.2 GHz clock speed
84.0 GB of system memory providing 42.0 GB of RAM per core
236 GB of local storage for temporary data and caching
Top-tier single-core performance with weak multi-core scaling
Suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded applications and text processing
